scenario: A team's process model has accumulated 12 versions across 6 months. Looking at v8, the team wants to compare it against v7 to see what changed.
question: Which Web Modeler feature shows version-to-version differences?

options.text: , VS Code diff view or a BPMN-aware comparison utility) to compare the raw XML and identify which elements changed between versions. Web Modeler's built-in version history provides a visual diff view and a code diff view without any export or external tooling. The built-in diff understands BPMN structure and highlights execution-affecting element changes, which raw XML diff cannot.

Web Modeler only shows the current version of a process model and does not maintain any version history. To compare previous versions, the team must maintain an external version control system like Git and commit BPMN files manually after each change. 7). The version history sidebar provides both Visual and Code diff views for any two versions without external tooling.
7) chronologically in the sidebar. md:43-80). DMN diagrams support comparison in Code view only. The team can compare v8 directly against v7 without any export steps.

Use Operate's process definition comparison view -- navigate to the process definition list in Operate, select two versions, and Operate renders a side-by-side BPMN diagram showing element-level differences between v7 and v8. Operate is a runtime monitoring tool that displays deployed process definition diagrams for navigation during instance investigation, but it does not provide version-to-version diff comparison. BPMN element-level version comparison is a design-time capability in Web Modeler's versioning feature, not an Operate feature.

a.text: Incorrect. Web Modeler's version history feature shows all versions chronologically and provides a diff view comparing any two versions -- visual highlighting of added, removed, or changed BPMN elements.

b.text: Incorrect. Web Modeler has built-in diff; external diff isn't needed.

c.text: Correct. Web Modeler maintains version history.

d.text: Incorrect. Operate doesn't have BPMN-element-level diff comparison; it's runtime monitoring.
